description: Phase K: multi-player households. Every scenario here declares a top-level "entities" list (in addition to the implicit primary) and asserts apply_as attribution — the one thing that distinguishes these from every other scenario in the sweep, which stay single-entity and (per the K4 validator) must never carry apply_as at all.

scenarios.name: Multi-Player - P1 at 5/24, apply attributed to P2
scenarios.description: The primary (Jamie) has 5 personal cards opened within 24 months, tripping Chase 5/24 for him specifically. Sam, a second household entity, has a clean history — the Chase candidate must still be recommended, attributed to Sam.

test_conditions: Jamie (primary) is at 5/24 via his 5 owned cards — blocked from applying
Sam has zero cards — clean 5/24 budget, eligible

scenarios.name: Multi-Player - both players at 5/24, card excluded
scenarios.description: Both the primary and Sam independently hold the same 5 cards (as their own copies), so BOTH are at 5/24 — the Chase candidate has no eligible entity left and must be excluded entirely, same as the single-player case.

test_conditions: Both Jamie and Sam independently hold 5 cards each -> both at 5/24
_eligible_entity_for_card returns None for every entity -> card silently excluded

scenarios.name: Multi-Player - business entity gets the business card
scenarios.description: A declared business entity (Acme LLC) is the one attributed on a business-card apply — business cards prefer a business entity over the personal primary.

test_conditions: card_type=business -> candidates are business entities first
Acme LLC is the only business entity -> gets the apply attribution

scenarios.name: Multi-Player - second-copy apply is bonus-only
scenarios.description: Jamie already holds and keeps a card; Sam applies for a fresh copy of the exact same card. Sam's copy is valued on its signup bonus alone (Jamie's copy already earns the category rewards).

test_conditions: Jamie's copy shows as KEEP with its normal category-rewards breakdown
Sam's copy shows as APPLY, valued on signup bonus minus fee alone
Reconciliation guard holds: Sam's breakdown line total is $0

scenarios.name: Multi-Player - lifetime application rule blocks per entity even after close
scenarios.description: Jamie held (and long ago closed) a card sharing a once-per-lifetime application family with a new candidate — he's blocked from ever applying again, even though the prior card is closed. Sam has no such history and is recommended the new card instead.

test_conditions: Jamie's once-per-lifetime application_eligibility family blocks him from A or B forever, even closed
Sam has a clean history -> eligible, gets the apply_as attribution for card B

scenarios.name: Multi-Player - Amex 5-card max blocks that entity, other eligible
scenarios.description: Jamie holds 5 open Amex cards (the issuer's own-card cap) and is blocked from a 6th. Sam has no Amex cards and is recommended the candidate instead.

test_conditions: Jamie holds 5 open Amex cards -> at the issuer's max_open_cards cap
Sam holds none -> eligible, gets the apply_as attribution
